assertions.description: HTMLGeolocationElement (or document.createElement('geolocation')) yields a non-Unknown element.
assertions.test: (()=>{const e=document.createElement('geolocation');return e.tagName.toLowerCase()==='geolocation' && (typeof HTMLGeolocationElement!=='undefined' ? e instanceof HTMLGeolocationElement : true);})()

assertions.description: The element supports an event handler for position-update.
assertions.test: (()=>{const e=document.createElement('geolocation');return 'onposition' in e || 'onpositionchange' in e || 'onresolve' in e;})()

assertions.description: navigator.geolocation API is unaffected by the addition of the declarative element.
The element is gated on a secure context; in secure contexts the constructor is exposed.
